title: Sanitize NaN, Infinite, -Infinite causing error when saving as PostgreSQL JSON
body:
## What type of PR is this? 
- [x] Bug Fix

## Description
When running query that output NaN, Infinite, or -Infinite, redash executor crashes with the error "Out of range float values are not JSON compliant" like below. Redash does not output any rows when the error was occurred.

```
[2025-02-19 04:48:49,040][PID:593][ERROR][rq.worker] [Job 6cfa1d1c-2a32-48c6-8bf4-0cd8f8ba6f32]: exception raised while executing (redash.tasks.queries.execution.execute_query)
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
sqlalchemy.exc.StatementError: ...
(builtins.ValueError) Out of range float values are not JSON compliant
[SQL: INSERT INTO query_results ...]
...
```

The error occurs because JSON(PostgreSQL JSON type) does not support NaN, Infinite, -Infinite.

This PR fixes the issue by sanitize NaN, Infinite, -Infinite value to None. This is the save behavior that JavaScript JSON.stringify() does.

We can reproduce the issue by running following query on BigQuery or PostgreSQL.

BigQuery:
```
select 1.0, cast('NaN' as float64), cast('Infinity' as float64), cast('-Infinity' as float64)
```

PostgreSQL:
```
select 1.0::float, 'NaN'::float, 'Infinity'::float, '-Infinity'::float
```

The issue was introduced by #6685 which removes simplejson. Before #6685 , NaN / Inf was converted to null by simplejson with ignore_nan=True. This PR change behavior for NaN/Inf as same as before #6685 .

## How is this tested?

- [x] Unit tests (pytest, jest)
- [x] Manually

Unit test was added.
Also, we can confirm by running the query on the description.

step_plan.summary: Prevent Redash query execution from crashing when result payloads contain NaN/Infinity/-Infinity by sanitizing non-finite floats to null (None) before JSON serialization into PostgreSQL JSON. The change restores the prior behavior from simplejson(ignore_nan=True) and adds/extends unit coverage for persistence of such results.

steps.operation: Add recursive sanitization of non-finite floats (NaN/+Inf/-Inf) to None before JSON serialization in json_dumps (while keeping allow_nan=False).
steps.reason: The crash originates from Python’s json.dumps raising ValueError when allow_nan=False and the payload contains non-finite floats; pre-sanitizing the object tree preserves strict JSON compliance and restores pre-#6685 behavior (simplejson(ignore_nan=True)).
steps.side_effects: All callers of redash.utils.json_dumps will now emit null for non-finite floats instead of raising; potential behavior change for any code relying on the exception. Must ensure recursion handles nested dict/list/tuple and doesn’t break custom encoder behavior.

steps.operation: Ensure DB binding for JSONText always uses the sanitized serialization path (and optionally add a defensive sanitize step if any other dump path exists).
steps.reason: QueryResult.data is persisted through JSONText TypeDecorator; guaranteeing it serializes via the updated json_dumps (or defensively sanitizing here too) prevents INSERT/flush failures even if other code paths pass non-finite floats into JSONText columns.
steps.side_effects: Affects all models/columns using JSONText; could mask non-finite float issues by coercing them to null at persistence time. Should keep allow_nan=False to avoid emitting invalid JSON into PostgreSQL JSON/JSONB.

steps.operation: Normalize stored QueryResult data by sanitizing before assignment/persistence so the in-memory value matches what is written to the DB.
steps.reason: Sanitizing at the persistence entrypoint makes behavior explicit and ensures any downstream use of the stored Python object (e.g., caching, comparison, later re-serialization) matches the DB representation and won’t re-trigger serialization failures.
steps.side_effects: Potential slight performance cost for walking large result payloads; may affect any code that expects NaN/Inf to remain floats in-memory after store_result. Requires updating/adding tests asserting values become None.
